per_question.answer: Jaro-Winkler similarity with a threshold of 0.92, requiring manual review.
The order status flow is: draft -> submitted -> processing -> shipped -> delivered. Terminal states also include cancelled and returned.
The maximum staleness allowed for inventory data is 5 minutes.
It is placed on auto-hold and an alert is triggered.

per_question.expected: Real-time via warehouse API (webhook + 5min poll fallback)
per_question.answer: Inventory is synced in real-time from a warehouse API, using a webhook with a 5-minute poll as a fallback.

per_question.expected: Yes - CUSTOMER says 36 months for churned, regulatory requires 7 years for financial records
per_question.answer: Yes, there is a known conflict between the customer retention policy (36 months for churned leads) and the regulatory requirement to retain financial records for 7 years. The 7-year regulatory retention overrides shorter entity-specific policies.
The email field is never null for active customers.

per_question.expected: Stored in UTC, displayed in customer locale
per_question.answer: Timestamps are stored in UTC and displayed according to the customer's locale.
Addresses are normalised using the SmartyStreets API and formatted for the US using USPS.
A minimum of 7 years, per regulatory requirements.

per_question.expected: auto-deactivated, reactivation requires manual review by merchandising
per_question.answer: Products with a 'seasonal' catalog status are automatically deactivated at the end of the season. Reactivation requires a manual review by the merchandising team.

per_question.expected: No, order_id is mandatory (PAYMENT belongs to ORDER)
per_question.answer: No. The context states that a Payment "Belongs To: Order (via order_id, mandatory)".
